report_metadata.step_description: NOTE: Only process documents with route_to_step=5 (document_category="Site Plan") — the same document set as step 5. For each Site Plan document processed in step 5, extract the complete drawing legend (key) from every page using the vision extraction pipeline defined in proc-extract-site-plan-info step 4. Scan every image chunk of each drawing for a LEGEND, KEY, NOTATION, or SYMBOL TABLE panel. Process every visible row in the legend from top to bottom — do NOT stop at the end of one section before checking for additional sections; many legends have 2–4 sections separated by dividers (cables, equipment, boundaries, duct arrangements). For each visual element in the legend extract: a plain-English description of the symbol or line style, the exact text label as written on the drawing, a category (one of: cable/equipment/substation/boundary/earthing/annotation/other — REQUIRED on every entry without exception; if uncertain use 'other'), and the page number it was found on. STRICT SCHEMA: each legend entry object must contain EXACTLY these four fields — page, symbol_description, label, category. STRICT FIELD PROHIBITION: Any field name other than these four is prohibited — including but not limited to legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, id, section, type, geometry, or any other key. Category 'earthing' applies to earth conductors and earth electrodes — the label EARTHING maps to category 'earthing', not 'other'. DUCT ARRANGEMENT SECTION: Some drawings have a duct cross-section diagram inside the legend panel with rows like DUCT WITH EXISTING CABLE, DUCT WITH NEW CABLE, NEW TRENCH, REMOVE CABLE, REMOVE DUCT & CABLE, REMOVE DUCT, SPARE DUCT, EARTHING, DUCT SPACING NTS — extract every one of these as a regular legend entry (category 'cable' for duct/trench/cable rows, 'earthing' for EARTHING, 'other' for DUCT SPACING NTS annotations). Assemble all entries across all chunks and all pages into a single structured list per document.
report_metadata.validation_criteria: Must return a JSON object with 'total_files' and 'extractions' array containing only Site Plan documents. Each extraction must include 'sub_step_extractions' with the key 'sub-step-extract-legend'. The value of 'sub-step-extract-legend' must be a non-empty JSON array of legend entry objects. Each entry must have EXACTLY four fields: 'page' (integer, 1-based), 'symbol_description' (non-empty string describing the visual symbol or line style — not a label paraphrase), 'label' (non-empty string — the exact verbatim text label from the drawing), and 'category' (string — REQUIRED on every entry, must be one of: cable, equipment, substation, boundary, earthing, annotation, other; NEVER null or absent — use 'other' if uncertain). Category MUST be assigned using pattern rules: 'cable' if label contains TRENCH/CABLE/CONDUCTOR/OVERHEAD MAINS/UNDERGROUND MAINS/DUCT; 'equipment' if label contains POLE/PILLAR/COLUMN/LANTERN/SLCP/SHACKLE/JOINT/UGOH/HV ABS/HV USL/LV LINK or is a short equipment code; 'substation' if label contains SUBSTATION/KIOSK; 'boundary' if label contains EASEMENT/DEMARCATION/BOUNDARY/LGA; 'earthing' if label is EARTHING; otherwise 'other'. Entries with extra fields beyond these four (such as legend_text, label_text, bounding_box, label_position, notes, or any other key) indicate schema drift and must be flagged. The array must contain at least one entry. Entries with null or empty symbol_description or label indicate incomplete extraction and must be flagged.
